meaning: adj.<美>高消费阶层的,迎合高层次消费者的,(商品)质优价高的
examples: She browsed in an upscale antiques shop.
A short history: Starbucks owned upscale consumers.
This fall the chain has opened outlets of upscale cosmetics purveyor Sephora in its stores.
But Wi is a bit more upscale and you may even spot celebrities like John Travolta.
